summary: Small heavily damaged register from Armatambo (Huaca San Pedro, Lima). 22 cords, 0 STRING, 13 L-1 (59% unreadable!). Total 197. W(13)/BG(4)/MB(4). 6 clusters. Essentially illegible — the most data-damaged khipu per percentage in the corpus.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  22 cords. 0 STRING, 8 INT, 1 EMPTY, 13 L-1 (59% unreadable).
  Architecture: 0-SEUL, L1. 6 clusters. Colors: W(13), BG(4), MB(4), AB(1).
  Provenance: Armatambo, Huaca San Pedro (Lima coast). Museum: Museo Nac., Pueblo Libre.
  Total: 197. 59% L-1 = most damaged per percentage in corpus.

NARRATIVE
  Thirteen of twenty-two cords are unreadable — NULL turns in the database, knots that disintegrated or were never recorded. What remains: eight numbers totaling 197, and a provenance that places this fragment at Armatambo, the great Ychma administrative center on the coast south of Lima. A ghost of a document, more absence than presence.
references: Armatambo/Huaca San Pedro — Ychma administrative center, Lima coast
